floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: replay/watch-mode HUD compositor (two find-state scans with sin/cos wobble, a 4x5 icon-grid cell loop with animated-vs-static split, then gauge/camera tail calls) -- func_80070C70 (

frontier.hypothesis: Drafting from the func_80070C70 template (PrimC70 + extended rgb/glyph fields, cheat-free spelling) + the m2c skeleton reproduces the bulk of the function; the shared extended-struct typedef also unblocks func_8005D814.
frontier.mechanism: same TU, same struct-on-stack layout and call chaining; GCC 2.7.2 emits identical prologue/struct-address patterns for identical shapes.
frontier.next_probe: Write the C body (plain constants where the template used pins/asm), sandbox func_8006F97C --disable all; then diff the residual against the template's known-friction spots (code=1 constant, saMotionSet(x, 0x60) mode reg).

frontier.hypothesis: The two find-state scans (==5 then ==4) are literal source duplication (copy-pasted block with two constants changed), not a helper function or loop -- their instruction sequences are byte-identical except the two constants and the x init.
frontier.mechanism: GCC cross-jumping cannot merge them (different constants embedded mid-block); a source helper would produce a jal instead.
frontier.next_probe: Draft as two pasted blocks; if register allocation diverges between copies, compare s0/s1 usage in the two windows to see which locals were re-declared vs reused (fresh scope per block vs shared).

frontier.hypothesis: The grid-cell skip condition compiles to the observed sltiu pair only when spelled as an unsigned range test on D_800A35BC (e.g. `if (D_800A35BC >= 2 && D_800A35BC - 2 < 2) goto draw;` or equivalently `if (!(u32)(D_800A35BC - 2) < 2)` shape) plus the col==4/row-odd test spelled with explicit `row == 1 || row == 3` (beq/beq, NOT row & 1).
frontier.mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 range-folds `v==2||v==3` to sltiu(v-2,2) but keeps `row==1||row==3` as two beqs when the values are not foldable to a mask test in this context (explicit-rejection-set-defeats-range-fold rule documents the inverse trap).
frontier.next_probe: A/B the spellings in the sandbox and diff the :300-319 window; consult explicit-rejection-set-defeats-range-fold if the row test folds when it should not.
